title: Fridericia carichanensis
intro: Fridericia carichanensis, also known as "Coralillo", is a plant species belonging to the Apocynaceae family, traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties. The health benefits of Fridericia carichanensis include its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antidiabetic properties, which have been reported to aid in the treatment of various conditions, such as wounds, infections, and diabetes. The bioactive constituents of this plant include iridoids, glycosides, and alkaloids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Fridericia carichanensis is often used in herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and extracts, which are commonly used to treat various health conditions.
taxonomy: Fridericia carichanensis belongs to the family Apocynaceae, commonly known as the dogbane or oleander family. This family is a large and diverse group of flowering plants that includes over 4,000 species. Within the Apocynaceae family, Fridericia carichanensis is classified as a species of the genus Fridericia. The genus Fridericia is a group of flowering plants native to the Americas, and it is characterized by its showy, funnel-shaped flowers. Fridericia carichanensis is a tropical plant species that is native to the subtropical regions of South America.
